text.title: AI-Assisted Interview Note Synthesis Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the interview-note-synthesis-ai methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every quote in the synthesis MUST cite (interview_id, line_range); unattributed quotes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unattributed quotes lose chain of custody and cannot be verified by re-readers.

rule.statement: A theme MUST be supported by ≥3 distinct interviewees to be labeled 'strong'; 2 = 'moderate'; 1 = 'individual-only', not a theme.

rationale.source: Lincoln & Guba triangulation criteria; UX research 2026 ops

rule.rationale: Single-source themes are anecdotes; the three-source rule is the minimum for triangulation.

rule.statement: Every theme MUST carry a confidence rating (H/M/L) computed from source count + agreement strength + counter-examples.
rule.rationale: Without confidence ratings, downstream readers cannot tell strong evidence from weak.

rule.statement: AI-generated synthesis MUST be reviewed by the researcher with a sign-off line in the report before any external sharing.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated quotes are the dominant AI-synthesis failure mode; the sign-off is the verification gate.

rule.statement: If N < 3 interviews OR sensitive content lacks redaction policy, skip and fall back to manual analysis.
rule.rationale: Three-source rule cannot apply below N=3; sensitive content without policy is a privacy hazard.
